---
title: "snakemake"
author: "AY"
date: '`r format(Sys.time(), "%d %B, %Y")`'
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

This repository uses `snakemake` for:

1. **Genome download + indexing** (STAR, bwa, bowtie2) with versioned index folders and stable symlinks.
2. **Mapping** using STAR, bwa, and/or bowtie2 with unified paired/single-end support.

<<<<<<< Updated upstream
<<<<<<< Updated upstream
For non-experts, prefer the single wrapper command `./scripts/bcfngs` (plan checks + build execution, local or SLURM).
=======
Use the wrapper command `./scripts/bcfngs`.
>>>>>>> Stashed changes
=======
For non-experts, prefer the single wrapper command `./scripts/bcfngs` (runs checks, then snakemake).
Default environment path used on the cluster:
`/fs/gpfs41/lv07/fileset07/home/b_cox/yeroslaviz/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes`
>>>>>>> Stashed changes

## Quickstart

```bash
<<<<<<< Updated upstream
# environment
./scripts/bcfngs env create
./scripts/bcfngs env check
=======
# 1) create/update env (only once, then as needed)
./scripts/bcfngs --env-prefix /fs/gpfs41/lv07/fileset07/home/b_cox/yeroslaviz/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes env create
./scripts/bcfngs --env-prefix /fs/gpfs41/lv07/fileset07/home/b_cox/yeroslaviz/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes env check
>>>>>>> Stashed changes

<<<<<<< Updated upstream
# 2) (optional) check if a newer Ensembl release exists
./scripts/bcfngs genomes check-updates

# 3) (optional) preflight checks + snakemake lint + dry-run
=======
# genomes
>>>>>>> Stashed changes
./scripts/bcfngs genomes plan
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 20

<<<<<<< Updated upstream
# 4b) force preflight checks + build in one command
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 20 --no-skip-plan

# 4c) run jobs through SLURM (instead of local execution)
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --executor slurm --jobs 20

# if SLURM executor is reported as unavailable, refresh the env first
./scripts/bcfngs env update

# 4d) override defaults for a specific cluster/queue when needed
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --executor slurm --jobs 20 --slurm-partition <other_partition>

# SLURM defaults used automatically in slurm mode:
# --partition p.hpcl8
# --mail-user yeroslaviz@biochem.mpg.de
# --mail-type ALL
# --time 04:00:00 (240 min)
# --cpus-per-task 20
# --mem-per-cpu 20G
=======
# mapping
./scripts/bcfngs mapping plan
./scripts/bcfngs mapping run
>>>>>>> Stashed changes
```

## Configuration files

1. Genome configuration: [`configs/genomes.yaml`](configs/genomes.yaml){target="_blank"}
2. Per-run mapping configuration: [`configs/run.yaml`](configs/run.yaml){target="_blank"}

## Genome workflow

Workflow file: [`workflow/genomes.smk`](workflow/genomes.smk){target="_blank"}

Examples:

```bash
<<<<<<< Updated upstream
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 20

# optional explicit preflight (or use --no-skip-plan on build)
./scripts/bcfngs genomes plan
```
=======
# all organisms
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 20
>>>>>>> Stashed changes

# subset
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 20 --organism Homo_sapiens,Mus_musculus
```

## Mapping workflow

Workflow file: [`workflow/mapping.smk`](workflow/mapping.smk){target="_blank"}

Mapping supports:

- `--mapper star,bwa,bowtie2` (default: `star`)
- `--paired-end` (default) / `--single-end`
- `samtools index`
- `deeptools` `bamCoverage` for bigWig

The old STAR-only Snakefiles were archived under `archive/legacy/mapping/`.

### Dry-run and execution

```bash
# default (paired-end, STAR)
./scripts/bcfngs mapping plan
./scripts/bcfngs mapping run

# one mapper
./scripts/bcfngs mapping run --mapper bwa

# all mappers
./scripts/bcfngs mapping run --mapper star,bwa,bowtie2
```

### Override config values for one run

```bash
<<<<<<< Updated upstream
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 20 --no-skip-plan
=======
./scripts/bcfngs mapping plan \
  --project P152 \
  --organism Drosophila_melanogaster \
  --fastq-dir /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/fastq_files/P152/conc.fastq \
  --outdir /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/mapping
>>>>>>> Stashed changes
```

### SLURM mode

```bash
<<<<<<< Updated upstream
./scripts/bcfngs mapping plan --executor slurm --jobs 20
./scripts/bcfngs mapping run  --executor slurm --jobs 20
=======
./scripts/bcfngs --env-prefix /fs/gpfs41/lv07/fileset07/home/b_cox/yeroslaviz/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes env update
./scripts/bcfngs --env-prefix /fs/gpfs41/lv07/fileset07/home/b_cox/yeroslaviz/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes env check
>>>>>>> Stashed changes
```

Default SLURM settings:

- partition `p.hpcl8`
- mail user `yeroslaviz@biochem.mpg.de`
- mail type `ALL`
- time `04:00:00`

### FASTQ pattern auto-detection

The workflow checks these patterns in order:

1. `*_R1.fastq.gz`
2. `*_R1_001.fastq.gz`
3. `*_L1_R1.fastq.gz`
4. `*.conc.R1.fastq.gz`

<<<<<<< Updated upstream
This still uses the micromamba env for python/snakemake, but loads vendor tools via modules:

```bash
module load bclconvert/4.3.13 star bowtie2 bwa
./scripts/bcfngs --profile modules env check
./scripts/bcfngs --profile modules genomes build --cores 20 --no-skip-plan
```

## Genome download + indexing

Workflow file: [workflow/genomes.smk](workflow/genomes.smk){target="_blank"}

### Mapping the samples with the `STAR`

[mapping the samples in a paired-end format](Star.MappingQuant.PairedEndFastq.Snakefile){target="_blank"}

This script contains three (four) steps. in the first step the `fastq` samples are mapped to the indexed genome. Then, they are being indexed. Last, after creating a chromosome-size file, the `bam` files are converted into `bigwig` files for better visualization using the [UCSC browser](http://genome.ucsc.edu/cgi-bin/hgTracks).

We have two separate scripts for single-end and paired-end runs. The PE run looks for both R1 and R2 fastq files, while the SE script will ignore all R2 samples. 

After the environment was created **and activated**, one must change to the directory of the concatenated fast files. E.g.

```bash
cd /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/fastq_files/P000/P000_Testrun/conc.fastq/
```

To run mapping, update `configs/run.yaml` in the repo, then run one of these Snakefiles.

```bash
# for single-end samples
snakemake -nps /path/to/repo/Star.MappingQuant.SingleEndFastq.Snakefile -j 50
# for paired-end samples
snakemake -nps /path/to/repo/Star.MappingQuant.PairedEndFastq.Snakefile -j 50
```

* `-n` - dry-run. This parameter will only output the command, but execute them. This should be used to test the parameters, but must be removed for the analysis to be done.
* `-p` -  Print the commands to be execute.
* `-s` - the name of the snakeake file to be executed. 
* `-j` - #cores. How many cores should be used in the run. 
=======
In paired-end mode, matching R2 files are required.
>>>>>>> Stashed changes
